info.title: OneKS API
info.description: This specification defines all RESTful operations for the OneKS system, including Kubernetes clusters and nodegroups.\n

port.description: Port where the OneKS API is listening

tags.name: clusters
tags.description: Create and manage OneKS Kubernetes clusters

get.description: Returns all registered cluster families that can be used when creating a cluster. Each family includes its supported Kubernetes versions, input definitions, and flavour catalog.
Returns the metadata for a single control plane family, including its flavour definitions and supported Kubernetes versions.

get.summary: Get input definitions for a cluster flavour
get.description: Returns the input schema for a cluster family and flavour. By default, flavour defaults are included in each user input definition. If `exclude_defaults=true` is provided, only the inputs without default value are returned.

parameters.name: exclude_defaults
parameters.description: When enabled, flavour inputs with default values are omitted

get.description: Returns every cluster document currently managed by OneKS.

post.description: Creates a new cluster document and the associated control plane group.

get.summary: Get deployment readiness check service status
get.description: Returns whether the OneKS deployment readiness check service is enabled in the OneKS server configuration.

enabled.description: Whether the OneKS readiness check service is enabled.

post.description: Runs the OneKS deployment readiness check service against a complete deployment placement before provisioning a Kubernetes cluster. The check validates the target OpenNebula cluster and networks, required OneKS appliance, and live bootstrap connectivity. The response is a Server-Sent Events stream. Each progress event uses the `check_cluster` event name and sends a JSON object inline in the event `data` field with the following structure: `name` is the check step, `state` is one of `started`, `success`, or `failure`, and `context` is an optional error or detail message.

schema.description: Server-Sent Events stream. Each event is named `check_cluster` and contains JSON data with `name`, `state`, and optional `context` fields.

post.description: Validates a OneKS deployment placement before creating a cluster. The operation checks that the target OpenNebula cluster exists, that the selected public and private networks belong to it, that the optional image datastore is available in it, and that the OneKS appliance required by the selected family is available. It does not create any OneKS resources.

valid.description: Whether the deployment placement can be used.

get.description: Returns a single cluster document. If `expand=true` is provided, `control_plane` and `node_groups` are expanded from lightweight references into full group bodies.

parameters.name: expand
parameters.description: Expand embedded group references into full group documents

patch.description: Updates the cluster document. Accepts fields such as `name` and `description`.

delete.description: Deletes a cluster. By default, OneKS starts the normal deprovisioning workflow. If `force=true` is supplied, the cluster is removed immediately from the database and the caller is responsible for manual infrastructure cleanup.

parameters.name: force
parameters.description: Skip the deprovisioning workflow and delete the cluster document directly

get.summary: Get cluster kubeconfig from the control plane
get.description: Returns the kubeconfig currently stored on the cluster control plane as a convenience accessor.

post.description: Retries recovery for a cluster that is in warning or a failure state. Recovery is only accepted when at least one associated group has a recoverable failure status.
Validates the requested Kubernetes version against the control plane family, updates the cluster document, and then starts the asynchronous upgrade workflow for the control plane and all nodegroups.

200.description: Cluster updated with the requested target version

get.description: Returns all registered nodegroup families that can be used to create worker pools inside a cluster.
Returns the metadata for a single nodegroup family, including flavour defaults and supported Kubernetes versions.

get.summary: Get input definitions for a nodegroup flavour
get.description: Returns the input schema for a nodegroup family and flavour. By default, flavour defaults are included in each user input definition. If `exclude_defaults=true` is provided, only the inputs without default value are returned.

parameters.name: exclude_defaults
parameters.description: When enabled, flavour inputs with default values are omitted

get.description: Returns all nodegroups attached to the specified cluster.

200.description: Array of nodegroups belonging to the cluster

post.summary: Create a nodegroup in a cluster
post.description: Creates a new worker nodegroup under the specified cluster and starts the provisioning workflow.

get.summary: Get a nodegroup from a cluster
get.description: Returns a single nodegroup from the specified cluster.

patch.description: Updates the nodegroup document. Accepts fields such as `name` and `description`.

delete.summary: Delete a nodegroup from a cluster
delete.description: Starts nodegroup deprovisioning. The cluster control plane cannot be deleted through this endpoint since they are managed by the cluster itself.

post.description: Retries recovery for a nodegroup that is in a recoverable failure state.
Sets the target worker count for the nodegroup and starts the scaling action. The request body must contain a non-negative integer in `target`.
Changes the permissions of the cluster document and recursively applies the same octet to the associated control plane and nodegroup documents.

octet.description: New permissions in octal format (for example, `640`).

post.description: Changes the owner of the cluster document and recursively applies the same ownership to the associated control plane and nodegroup documents.

group_id.description: Optional new group ID. If omitted, the current group is preserved.

post.description: Changes the group ownership of the cluster document and recursively applies the same group to the associated control plane and nodegroup documents.

Cluster.description: OpenNebula JSON document representing a OneKS cluster

ID.description: ID of the resource in OpenNebula

PERMISSIONS.description: Permission bits for the object (U: Use, M: Manage, A: Admin)

state.description: Current state of the cluster lifecycle

kubernetes_version.description: Desired Kubernetes version for the cluster

control_plane.description: Control plane reference or expanded control plane group body. When expanded, control-plane-specific attributes such as `endpoint` may be present.

node_groups.description: Nodegroups attached to the cluster. Entries are references in list mode and full group bodies when expanded.

registration_time.description: Unix timestamp indicating when the cluster was registered

historic.description: Historic lifecycle events registered for the cluster

K8sGroup.description: OpenNebula JSON document representing a OneKS Kubernetes group, either a control plane or a nodegroup

ID.description: ID of the resource in OpenNebula

PERMISSIONS.description: Permission bits for the object (U: Use, M: Manage, A: Admin)

state.description: Current state of the group lifecycle

vms.description: OpenNebula VM IDs currently associated with the group

dependencies.description: Dependencies tracked by the group lifecycle manager

user_inputs_values.description: Resolved input values that define the desired size and capacity of the group

registration_time.description: Unix timestamp indicating when the group was registered

historic.description: Historic lifecycle events registered for the group

endpoint.description: Kubernetes API endpoint. This attribute is only populated for control plane groups.

K8sGroupReference.description: Compact group reference stored inside the cluster document when full body is not requested.

K8sDependency.description: Runtime dependency tracked by a Kubernetes group.

type.description: Whether the dependency is managed internally or tracked as external

id.description: ID of the associated OpenNebula resource when available

ready.description: Indicates whether the dependency is ready for use

action.description: Short action name recorded in the lifecycle history

K8sFamily.description: Family metadata for either a control plane family or a nodegroup family.

family.description: Family identifier used in API requests

supported_k8s_versions.description: Kubernetes versions accepted by this family

user_inputs.description: Input definitions available for all flavours in the family

dependencies.description: Dependency definitions declared by the family

UserInput.description: User input definition exposed by a OneKS family or flavour.

mandatory.description: Whether the input must be provided by the caller

default.description: Default value injected by the selected flavour when applicable

K8sFlavour.description: Preset resource profile for a family.

description.description: Human-readable description, which may contain HTML fragments

override_defaults.description: Whether user-supplied values are allowed to override flavour defaults

defaults.description: Default values applied to user inputs when this flavour is selected

K8sDependencyDefinition.description: Dependency declared statically by a family definition.

opts.description: Dependency options loaded from the family configuration

user_inputs.description: Resolved user input definitions for the selected flavour

options.description: Behaviour flags for the selected flavour

override_defaults.description: Whether caller-provided values override the flavour defaults

ClusterControlPlaneSpec.description: Control plane family and flavour selection used during cluster creation.

name.description: Optional control plane group name override

description.description: Optional control plane group description override

family.description: Control plane family. When omitted, the backend defaults to `general`.

user_inputs_values.description: Explicit values for the selected control plane flavour inputs

ClusterUpdateRequest.description: Update payload accepted by the cluster controller.

kubernetes_version.description: Target Kubernetes version. It must be listed in the selected control plane family's `supported_k8s_versions`.

family.description: Nodegroup family. When omitted, the backend defaults to `general`.

user_inputs_values.description: Explicit values for the selected nodegroup flavour inputs

NodeGroupUpdateRequest.description: Update payload accepted by the nodegroup controller.

kubeconfig.description: Kubeconfig content encoded as YAML text, sourced from the control plane group.

ClusterDeployment.description: OpenNebula placement used by a OneKS cluster deployment.

cluster.description: Target OpenNebula cluster where OneKS resources are deployed.

networks.description: Virtual Networks used by the OneKS cluster.

public.description: Public Virtual Network used for external access and bootstrap connectivity.

private.description: Private Virtual Network used for internal Kubernetes communication.

204.description: The request has been accepted for processing

405.description: The requested action is not allowed in the current resource state

500.description: The server encountered an unexpected condition which prevented it from fulfilling the request.
